Description:
A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation that provides access to the AACT (Aggregate Analysis of ClinicalTrials.gov https://aact.ctti-clinicaltrials.org/) database using the FastMCP framework. This server allows AI assistants to directly query clinical trial data from the ClinicalTrials.gov database.
Here are some example prompts to use with this plugin:

"What are the most common types of interventions in breast cancer clinical trials?"
"How many phase 3 clinical trials were completed in 2023?"
"Show me the enrollment statistics for diabetes trials across different countries"
"What percentage of oncology trials have reported results in the last 5 years?"

Features
Tools
list_tables

Get an overview of all available tables in the AACT database
Useful for understanding the database structure before analysis
describe_table

Examine the detailed structure of a specific AACT table
Shows column names and data types
Example: {"table_name": "studies"}
read_query

Use Case: "Use the AWS CLI to list all S3 buckets in the us-east-1 region and flag any that do not have public access blocking enabled."

Features
Natural language to Postgres SQL query
Converting human-readable questions and commands into structured Postgres-compatible SQL queries and executing them against the configured Aurora Postgres database.

This AWS Labs Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for CloudTrail enables your AI agents to query AWS account activity for security investigations, compliance auditing, and operational troubleshooting. Use Case: "Query CloudTrail Lake for all 'AccessDenied' events associated with the 'Admin' user over the last 48 hours and identify the source IP addresses."
Instructions
The CloudTrail MCP Server provides specialized tools to address common security and operational scenarios including event lookup, user activity analysis, API call tracking, and advanced CloudTrail Lake analytics. Each tool encapsulates one or multiple CloudTrail APIs into task-oriented operations.

Features
Event Lookup - Search CloudTrail events by various attributes including username, event name, resource name, and more. Provides access to the last 90 days of management events for security investigations and troubleshooting.

CloudTrail Lake Analytics - Execute advanced SQL queries against CloudTrail Lake for complex analytics, filtering, and aggregation. Supports Trino-compatible SQL syntax for comprehensive event analysis.

User Activity Analysis - Track and analyze user activities across AWS services by filtering events by username, access key, or other user-related attributes.

API Call Tracking - Monitor specific API calls and their patterns across your AWS environment for security and compliance purposes.

Event Data Store Management - List and explore available CloudTrail Lake Event Data Stores to understand data sources and capabilities.

An AWS Labs Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Aurora DSQL and corresponding AI rules that can be used for additional model steering while developing.

Features
Converting human-readable questions and commands into structured Postgres-compatible SQL queries and executing them against the configured Aurora DSQL database.
Read-only by default, transactions enabled with --allow-writes
Connection reuse between requests for improved performance
Built-in access to Aurora DSQL documentation, search, and best practice recommendations
Available Tools
Database Operations
[IMPORTANT] The MCP Server requires a valid configuration for --cluster_endpoint, --database_user, and --region to enable database operations.

readonly_query - Execute read-only SQL queries against your DSQL cluster
transact - Execute SQL statements in a transaction
In read-only mode: Supports read operations with transactional consistency
With --allow-writes: Supports all write operations too
get_schema - Retrieve table schema information
Documentation and Recommendations
dsql_search_documentation - Search Aurora DSQL documentation
Parameters: search_phrase (required), limit (optional)
dsql_read_documentation - Read specific DSQL documentation pages
Parameters: url (required), start_index (optional), max_length (optional)
dsql_recommend - Get recommendations for DSQL best practices
Parameters: url (required)

The official developer experience MCP Server for Amazon DynamoDB. This server provides DynamoDB expert design guidance and data modeling assistance.

Available Tools
The DynamoDB MCP server provides seven tools for data modeling, validation, and code generation:

dynamodb_data_modeling - Retrieves the complete DynamoDB Data Modeling Expert prompt with enterprise-level design patterns, cost optimization strategies, and multi-table design philosophy. Guides through requirements gathering, access pattern analysis, and schema design.

Example invocation: "Design a data model for my e-commerce application using the DynamoDB data modeling MCP server"

dynamodb_data_model_validation - Validates your DynamoDB data model by loading dynamodb_data_model.json, setting up DynamoDB Local, creating tables with test data, and executing all defined access patterns. Saves detailed validation results to dynamodb_model_validation.json.

Example invocation: "Validate my DynamoDB data model"

source_db_analyzer - Analyzes existing MySQL databases to extract schema structure, access patterns from Performance Schema, and generates timestamped analysis files for use with dynamodb_data_modeling. Supports both RDS Data API-based access and connection-based access.

Example invocation: "Analyze my MySQL database and help me design a DynamoDB data model"

generate_resources - Generates various resources from the DynamoDB data model JSON file (dynamodb_data_model.json). Currently only the cdk resource type is supported. Passing cdk as resource_type parameter generates a CDK app to deploy DynamoDB tables. The CDK app reads the dynamodb_data_model.json to create tables with proper configuration.

Example invocation: "Generate the resources to deploy my DynamoDB data model using CDK"

dynamodb_data_model_schema_converter - Converts your data model (dynamodb_data_model.md) into a structured schema.json file representing your DynamoDB tables, indexes, entities, fields, and access patterns. This machine-readable format is used for code generation and can be extended for other purposes like documentation generation or infrastructure provisioning. Automatically validates the schema with up to 8 iterations to ensure correctness.

Example invocation: "Convert my data model to schema.json for code generation"

dynamodb_data_model_schema_validator - Validates schema.json files for code generation compatibility. Checks field types, operations, GSI mappings, pattern IDs, and provides detailed error messages with fix suggestions. Ensures your schema is ready for the generate_data_access_layer tool.

Example invocation: "Validate my schema.json file at /path/to/schema.json"

generate_data_access_layer - Generates type-safe Python code from schema.json including entity classes with field validation, repository classes with CRUD operations, fully implemented access patterns, and optional usage examples. The generated code uses Pydantic for validation and boto3 for DynamoDB operations.

Example invocation: "Generate Python code from my schema.json"

An MCP server for containerizing applications, deploying applications to Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS), troubleshooting ECS deployments, and managing ECS resources. This server enables AI assistants to help users with the full lifecycle of containerized applications on AWS.

Note: AWS offers a fully managed Amazon ECS MCP server that provides enterprise-grade capabilities including automatic updates, centralized security through IAM integration, comprehensive audit logging via CloudTrail, and the proven scalability and reliability of AWS. The managed service eliminates the need for local installation and maintenance.

Features
Containerization Guidance: Provides best practices and guidance for containerizing web applications
ECS Express Mode Deployment: Deploy containerized applications using ECS Express Mode with automatic infrastructure provisioning
ECR Integration: Automated ECR repository creation and Docker image builds with push to ECR
Load Balancer Integration: Automatically configure Application Load Balancers (ALBs) with HTTPS support
Auto-scaling: Built-in auto-scaling with configurable CPU/memory and scaling targets
Infrastructure as Code: Generate and apply CloudFormation templates for ECR and ECS infrastructure
URL Management: Return public ALB URLs for immediate access to deployed applications
Circuit Breaker: Implement deployment circuit breaker with automatic rollback
Container Insights: Enable enhanced container insights for monitoring
Security Best Practices: Implement AWS security best practices for container deployments
Resource Management: List and explore ECS resources such as task definitions, services, clusters, and tasks
AWS Knowledge Integration: Access up-to-date AWS documentation through the integrated AWS Knowledge MCP Server proxy which includes knowledge on ECS and new features released that models may not be aware of
Customers can use the containerize_app tool to help them containerize their applications with best practices. The build_and_push_image_to_ecr tool creates ECR infrastructure and pushes Docker images. The validate_ecs_express_mode_prerequisites tool validates that all required IAM roles and images exist before deployment. Customers deploy using ecs_resource_management with the CreateExpressGatewayService operation for Express Mode deployments. The wait_for_service_ready tool helps track deployment progress, and delete_app provides complete cleanup of Express Mode deployments.

Customers can list and view their ECS resources (clusters, services, tasks, task definitions) and access their ECR resources (container images) using the ecs_resource_management tool. When running into ECS deployment issues, they can use the ecs_troubleshooting_tool to diagnose and resolve common problems.

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Amazon MQ that enables generative AI models to manage RabbitMQ and ActiveMQ message brokers through MCP tools.

Features
This MCP server acts as a bridge between MCP clients and Amazon MQ, allowing generative AI models to create, configure, and manage message brokers. Furthermore, it provides tools to manage Amazon MQ for RabbitMQ brokers at the broker level. The server provides a secure way to interact with Amazon MQ resources while maintaining proper access controls and resource tagging.


From a security perspective, this server implements resource tagging to ensure that only resources created through the MCP server can be modified by it. This prevents unauthorized modifications to existing Amazon MQ resources that were not created by the MCP server.

Key Capabilities
Create and manage Amazon MQ brokers (RabbitMQ and ActiveMQ)
Configure broker settings and parameters
List and describe existing brokers
Reboot and update brokers
Create and manage broker configurations
Automatic resource tagging for security

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AWS Step Functions to choose and run state machines as MCP tools without code changes.

This MCP server acts as a bridge between MCP clients and AWS Step Functions state machines, allowing generative AI models to access and run state machines as tools. This enables seamless integration with existing Step Function workflows without requiring any modifications to their definitions. Through this bridge, AI models can execute and manage complex, multi-step business processes that coordinate operations across multiple AWS services.

The server supports both Standard and Express workflows, adapting to different execution needs. Standard workflows excel at long-running processes where status tracking is essential, while Express workflows handle high-volume, short-duration tasks with synchronous execution. This flexibility ensures optimal handling of various workflow patterns and requirements.

To ensure data quality and provide clear documentation, the server integrates with EventBridge Schema Registry for input validation. It combines schema information with state machine definitions to generate comprehensive tool documentation, helping AI models understand both the purpose and technical requirements of each workflow.

From a security perspective, the server implements IAM-based authentication and authorization, creating a clear separation of duties. While models can invoke state machines through the MCP server, they don't have direct access to other AWS services. Instead, the state machines themselves handle AWS service interactions using their own IAM roles, maintaining robust security boundaries while enabling powerful workflow capabilities.

Tool Name: AWS Well Architectured Security mcp server
Description:

Features

Security Operations Dashboard: Retrieve and analyze security findings from AWS services for operational visibility
Compliance Operations: Continuously assess security posture against Well-Architected Framework for operational compliance
Resource Operations: Discover and monitor AWS resources across multiple services and regions for security operations
Cost-Effective Data Protection: Monitor storage configuration for encryption compliance while optimizing security costs
Network Operations Security: Verify network configuration for encryption compliance in operational environments
Compliance Monitoring: Monitor compliance status of AWS resources against security standards for operational reporting
Security Operations Context: Access stored security context data for operational analysis and trending
Operations teams can use the CheckSecurityServices tool to monitor if critical AWS security services are operational across their infrastructure. The GetSecurityFindings tool provides operational visibility into security findings, while AnalyzeSecurityPosture delivers comprehensive security operations reporting against the Well-Architected Framework. The ExploreAwsResources tool provides operational inventory capabilities across services and regions to ensure complete operational visibility and cost optimization of the AWS environment.

Company enrichment via AbstractAPI. Returns organization metadata for a domain. Company enrichment and firmographic data retrieval service via AbstractAPI. Outputs: JSON-formatted organization metadata including legal name, industry classification, global office locations, staff size (headcount), year founded, and corporate status. Capabilities: Real-time data updates, global coverage (170+ countries), GDPR/CCPA compliance, and sub-second response times. Use Cases: B2B lead qualification, dynamic form shortening, market research, risk assessment, and customer segmentation.

Keywords: Firmographics, B2B data, Lead Scoring, CRM Enrichment, Organization Metadata, Domain Lookup, Market Intelligence.

Data Fields: name, domain, year_founded, industry, employees_count, locality, country, type.

API key via Environment Variables: COMPANY_ENRICHMENT_API_KEY.

Get your key here: https://www.abstractapi.com/
Author: Hackerdogs

Description:
Email reputation and deliverability checks via AbstractAPI. 
A comprehensive email verification and deliverability tool. It performs real-time checks to determine if an email address is valid, active, and safe to contact. It identifies disposable (burner) addresses, free providers (Gmail/Yahoo), and catch-all configurations.

Keywords: email validation, deliverability, SMTP check, spam prevention, disposable email detection, MX records, email bounce reduction, lead cleaning.

Use Case: Use this when a user needs to verify a signup, clean a mailing list, or assess the risk level of a specific email address.


API key via Environment Variables: EMAIL_REPUTATION_API_KEY.

Get your key here: https://www.abstractapi.com/
Author: Hackerdogs

Description:
Live exchange rates via AbstractAPI. Returns base currency and exchange_rates map. 
Provides real-time and historical global currency conversion data. It returns the base currency and a mapped object of current exchange rates against dozens of international currencies.

Keywords: currency conversion, forex rates, FX, money exchange, base currency, live market rates, financial data, international pricing.

Use Case: Ideal for calculating prices in different currencies, monitoring market fluctuations, or performing automated currency conversions in checkout flows.

API key via Environment variables: EXCHANGE_RATES_API_KEY.
Author: Hackerdogs

Description:
Holiday lookup via AbstractAPI. Returns holiday objects for a country and date. 
A global holiday lookup service that identifies public, bank, and religious holidays for specific countries and dates. It returns metadata including the holiday name, localized name, and type (e.g., national vs. regional).

Keywords: public holidays, calendar events, bank holidays, observances, country schedule, holiday API, vacation planning.

API key via Environment variables: HOLIDAYS_API_KEY.
Author: Hackerdogs

Description:
IBAN validation via AbstractAPI. Validates IBAN and returns validity details. 
Validates International Bank Account Numbers (IBAN) for accuracy and authenticity. It checks the mathematical checksum and returns details about the associated bank, branch code, and country.

Keywords: IBAN check, bank account verification, SWIFT/BIC, payment validation, SEPA, wire transfer security, financial compliance.

API key via environment variables: IBAN_VALIDATION_API_KEY.
Author: Hackerdogs

Description:
IP intelligence via AbstractAPI. Returns geo/ASN/security signals for an IP. 
Provides deep insights into IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. It returns geolocation (city, country, coordinates), ASN data (Internet Service Provider), and security signals such as VPN, proxy, or Tor detection.

Description:

Description:

Amass MCP supports the following parameters:

subcommand: The Amass operation mode to use (required)

enum: Perform subdomain enumeration and network mapping
intel: Gather intelligence about target domains from various sources
For enum subcommand:

domain: Target domain to perform reconnaissance against (required)
enum_type: Enumeration approach type (optional)
active: Includes DNS resolution and potential network interactions with target
passive: Only uses information from third-party sources without direct target interaction

enum_brute_wordlist: Path to custom wordlist file for brute force operations (optional)

domain: Target domain to gather intelligence on (optional if organization is provided)

User: Perform reconnaissance on example.com

After configuring your integration, test it by prompting your agent with a question like "What services are running on 8.8.8.8?"

The output may depend on your specific configuration, but your agent should use a GET HOST Platform API endpoint to retrieve information about the host and provide a human-readable summary.

Example use cases
The MCP Server currently works best for retrieving and summarizing information about individual or bulk web assets and pivoting from information obtained from those lookups.

Investigate and pivot from a suspicious IP or web property
Use your AI agent to look up details about the host or web property a system in your environment has connected to using input like "what services are running on 1.1.1.1?"
If the asset appears unremarkable in its current state, instruct your agent to investigate the asset's history in a time frame that interests you.
If you obtain values of interest, instruct your agent to perform additional searches using those values to find related infrastructure. For example, prompt it to find other host services or web properties running the same certificates that you found on your first host or web property.
If you are able to identify indicators of interest, instruct your agent to build a collection targeting those indicators. Then, set up alerts to receive notifications about changes to the collection.
Gain visibility into exposed assets
Instruct your agent to search your organization's CIDR.
Identify host services associated with your organization that should not be exposed.
Use your agent to investigate the history of a host with an exposed service to identify when the service was exposed.
Remediate the exposure and consider using your agent to build a collection monitor for additional exposures.
Platform MCP tools
In addition to functionality that is equivalent to the Platform APIs, the Platform MCP server features the following tools.

Tools
retrieve_cve_details: Discovers which internet-facing systems are affected by security vulnerabilities. Uses the CVE ID format CVE-YYYY-NNNNN.

validate_censys_query: Checks field names, suggests corrections, and validates search query structure to avoid syntax errors.

search_field_help: Discovers relevant fields for your search when unsure about exact field names.

get_query_examples: Uses proven, working examples of Platform queries to learn proper query syntax and find starting points for searches with validated examples.

list_available_data_definition_groups: List all available data schema groups (such as host, certificate, or web property) that define searchable fields in the Platform to understand what types of data you can search and analyze, organized by asset type.

list_available_data_definitions:Shows all searchable field names available for a data type.

get_data_definition: Provides comprehensive documentation of a field including description, data type, and usage examples.

generate_query: Converts natural language requests into valid CenQL queries.

investigate_cve: Investigates a specific CVE vulnerability across the internet to provide comprehensive threat intelligence.
investigate_host: Conducts comprehensive security analysis of a specific IP address.

discover_attack_surface: Automatically searches for organization assets, analyzes exposed services, identifies security risks, and provides comprehensive attack surface assessment.

generate_and_search_query: Generates a Censys search query from natural language and searches the query.

Build a certificate relationship graph for a host and return JSON output. Uses Dockerized certgraph. Best default driver is http (direct TLS fetch). Use crtsh driver when you want CT-based expansion (can be large and occasionally flaky).

A reconnaissance tool that builds a directed relationship graph of SSL/TLS certificates for a target host. It maps "chains of trust" by identifying Subject Alternative Names (SANs) and re-used certificates. It supports the http driver for direct fetching and the crtsh driver for expanding searches via public Certificate Transparency (CT) logs.

Search Keywords: SSL certificate graph, subdomain enumeration, certificate transparency logs, SAN mapping, infrastructure relationships, crt.sh driver, TLS fingerprinting.

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects AI coding agents (such as Claude, Gemini, or Copilot) to a live Chrome browser. It leverages the Chrome DevTools Protocol to enable automation, in-depth debugging, performance analysis, and console inspection.

Tools Provided:

click: Clicks an element on the page.

drag: Drags an element to a new location.

fill: Fills an input field with text.

fill_form: Fills multiple form fields automatically.

handle_dialog: Handles JavaScript alerts, confirms, and prompts.

hover: Hovers the cursor over an element.

press_key: Simulates a keyboard key press.

upload_file: Uploads a file to a file input element.

close_page: Closes the current browser tab or page.

list_pages: Lists all open browser tabs.

Maps_page: Navigates the current tab to a specific URL.

new_page: Opens a new browser tab.

select_page: Switches focus to a specific tab.

wait_for: Waits for a specific condition or element to appear.

emulate: Emulates a specific device viewport and user agent.

resize_page: Resizes the browser window.

performance_analyze_insight: Analyzes performance traces to provide actionable insights.

performance_start_trace: Starts recording a performance trace.

performance_stop_trace: Stops recording and saves the performance trace.

get_network_request: Retrieves details about a specific network request.

list_network_requests: Lists network requests made by the page.

evaluate_script: Executes arbitrary JavaScript in the browser context.

get_console_message: Retrieves a specific console message.

list_console_messages: Lists messages logged to the browser console.

take_screenshot: Captures a screenshot of the current page.

take_snapshot: Captures the current DOM snapshot of the page.

A comprehensive browser automation and debugging server that connects AI agents to a live Chrome instance. Overview
edu-data-mcp-server is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides access to the Urban Institute's Education Data API. It enables AI assistants to query school and district enrollment data, college/university data, assessment and financial data, and more, organized by levels (schools, school-districts, college-university), sources (ccd, ipeds, crdc, etc.), and topics (enrollment, directory, finance, etc.).

Description
This MCP server exposes the Education Data API through structured tools and resources. It supports detailed data retrieval with filters (year, grade, fips, etc.) and aggregated summaries (sum, avg, count, median) grouped by variables. The server is designed for researchers, analysts, and developers who need education statistics for K–12 and higher education in the United States.

Key Features
get_education_data – Retrieve detailed education data with optional subtopics (e.g. race, sex) and filters (year, grade, fips, etc.)
get_education_data_summary – Aggregate data by statistic (sum, avg, count, median) and grouping variables
Resources – Browse endpoint info via edu-data://endpoints/{level}/{source}/{topic}
No API Key Required – Urban Institute Education Data API is publicly accessible
Tools Provided
1. get_education_data
Retrieve detailed education data from the API.

Parameters:

level (required): e.g. schools, school-districts, college-university
source (required): e.g. ccd, ipeds, crdc
topic (required): e.g. enrollment, directory
subtopic (optional): e.g. ['race', 'sex']
filters (optional): e.g. { year: 2020, grade: [9,10,11,12], fips: 6 }
add_labels (optional): default false
limit (optional): default 100
2. get_education_data_summary
Retrieve aggregated education data.

Parameters:

level, source, topic (required)
subtopic (optional)
stat (required): e.g. sum, avg, count, median
var (required): variable to summarize
by (required): variables to group by
filters (optional)

What is Globalping?
Globalping is a free, public API that provides access to a globally distributed network of probes for monitoring, debugging, and benchmarking internet infrastructure. With Globalping, you can run network tests (ping, traceroute, DNS, MTR, HTTP) from thousands of locations worldwide.

What is the Globalping MCP Server?
The Globalping MCP Server implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing AI models like OpenAI's GPT and Anthropic's Claude to interact with Globalping's network measurement capabilities through natural language.

It also supports two authentication methods: OAuth and API token authentication. Both methods offer a secure way to interact with our API and provide higher rate limits associated with your account.

A network diagnostic server that provides access to a globally distributed network of thousands of probes. It allows AI agents to run real-time network tests—including ping, traceroute, DNS (dig), MTR, and HTTP—from specific geographic locations. It is essential for debugging latency, troubleshooting global DNS propagation, or benchmarking internet infrastructure performance.

Search Keywords: global network monitoring, latency testing, worldwide ping, traceroute analysis, DNS propagation check, MTR diagnostics, HTTP benchmarking, Globalping API.

Use Case: Benchmarking the website loading speed from Tokyo, London, and New York simultaneously to identify regional latency issues.

Key Features
🌐 Global Network Access: Run measurements from thousands of probes worldwide
🤖 AI-Friendly Interface: Any LLM will easily parse the data and run new measurements as needed
📊 Comprehensive Measurements: Support for ping, traceroute, DNS, MTR, and HTTP tests
🔍 Smart Context Handling: Provides detailed parameter descriptions for AI clients to intelligently select measurement types and options
🔄 Comparative Analysis: Allows to compare network performance between different targets
🔑 Authentication Support: Use OAuth or API token with your Globalping account for higher rate limits

Description:
A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects AI assistants to the GreyNoise Enterprise API. It enables comprehensive analysis of internet-scanning IPs, threat actors, and vulnerabilities, providing tools for IP context lookups, tag exploration, and vulnerability tracking to enhance threat intelligence workflows.

A threat intelligence server that connects to the GreyNoise Enterprise API to analyze internet-scanning traffic. It differentiates between "noise" (benign scanners like Google or Shodan) and targeted malicious activity. Its "RIOT" tool identifies common business services (SaaS, CDNs), while its vulnerability tracking identifies CVEs currently being exploited in the wild.

Search Keywords: IP intelligence, internet scanning noise, threat actor tracking, RIOT business services, vulnerability exploitation, GNQL query, malicious IP classification, CVE exploitation.

Use Case: Investigating a suspicious IP address to see if it is a known malicious scanner or simply a benign cloud service like Microsoft 365.

Tools Provided:

lookup-ip-context: Retrieves detailed context information about a specific IP address.

quick-check-ip: Performs a fast, lightweight check to determine if an IP is scanning the internet or is a common business service.

multi-ip-check: Checks multiple IP addresses (up to 100) simultaneously for noise status and common business services.

riot-lookup: Checks if an IP belongs to a common business service (RIOT) and retrieves detailed service information.

get-tag-list: Retrieves the complete list of GreyNoise tags with metadata (identifiers, descriptions, categories).

search-tags: Searches for GreyNoise tags matching a specific query string.

get-tag-details: Retrieves comprehensive metadata for a specific tag.

get-tag-activity: Retrieves activity data for a specific tag, including trends over time.

analyze-tags-activity: Analyzes activity patterns across multiple tags to identify correlations.

get-trending-vulnerabilities: Retrieves information on vulnerabilities actively being exploited in the wild.

gnql-stats: Queries the GreyNoise database using the GreyNoise Query Language (GNQL) to retrieve statistical data.

get-cve-details: Retrieves detailed information about a specific CVE, including GreyNoise observations of exploitation.

Hackerdogs transforms public, open, and adversarial signals into validated intelligence about emerging risks forming outside your organization. The platform automates discovery, collection, and validation across public and semi public sources including breach disclosures, dark web marketplaces, forums, social platforms, leaked datasets, and industry specific feeds. Findings are correlated, de duplicated, and supported with evidence so teams can act on OSINT with confidence rather than speculation.
With Hackerdogs MCP server you can schedule OSINT, Threat, Vulnerabilities and Threat Research related prompts. 

1. Summarize today's high-severity alerts from our SIEM. List the top 10 by count, include affected assets and recommended actions. Keep it to one page.

2. List all AWS resources in us-east-1 that do not have the required compliance tags (Environment, Owner, CostCenter). Group by resource type and account. Output as a table.

3. Check VirusTotal and abuse.ch for these indicators and summarize the verdicts: [paste IOCs]. For each, say malicious/clean/unknown and one-line context. Flag any that appear in both sources.

4. Query CloudTrail for all AccessDenied events in the last 24 hours. Group by user/role and source IP. List the top 10 callers and the APIs they were denied on. Suggest whether this looks like misconfiguration or possible abuse.

5. List open critical and high vulnerabilities for our web applications from the last scan. For each, give CVE, affected asset, and one recommended mitigation. Prioritize by CVSS and whether exploit code is public.

6. Analyze our application logs for the last 6 hours. Identify anomalies: error spikes, new error types, or unusual request patterns. Summarize in bullet points with timestamps and example log lines.

7. For this incident summary: [paste summary]. Map the described TTPs to MITRE ATT&CK. List technique IDs, names, and which part of the incident they match. Suggest 2–3 mitigations from ATT&CK for the top techniques.

8. Give a one-page snapshot: (1) top 5 cost drivers in our AWS accounts this month, (2) any S3 buckets or RDS instances that are publicly accessible or missing encryption, (3) IAM roles with overly broad permissions. Use our cloud tools and summarize in clear bullets.

9. Analyze these URLs for phishing or malware: [paste URLs]. Use available threat-intel and web tools. For each URL, give: safe/suspicious/malicious, reason, and source. If any are malicious, suggest containment steps.

10. Re-analyze the conversation we had about [incident ID / topic]. Consider any new context from the last 24 hours (new IOCs, new alerts, or policy changes). Summarize what's changed and whether the original assessment still holds.

Julius fingerprints 33+ LLM services running on network endpoints. It sends crafted HTTP probes and matches response signatures to identify the exact server infrastructure — Ollama, vLLM, LiteLLM, HuggingFace TGI, LocalAI, LM Studio, NVIDIA NIM, llama.cpp, Aphrodite, FastChat, GPT4All, KoboldCpp, gateway proxies, RAG platforms, and more.


Summary. MCP server wrapper for Julius — LLM service fingerprinting tool by Praetorian.

Tools:

probe_targets — Probe one or more target URLs to fingerprint which LLM service is running. Detects 33+ services including Ollama, vLLM, LiteLLM, HuggingFace TGI, LocalAI, LM Studio, NVIDIA NIM, and more.
list_probes — List all available probe definitions that Julius can use for fingerprinting LLM services.
validate_probes — Validate custom probe definition files (YAML/JSON).
download_file — Download a file or repository from a URL into the container workspace. Use this to pre-download content before running multiple analyses on the same data.
cleanup_downloads — Remove downloaded files from the container workspace.

Example Prompts
"Scan http://192.168.1.100:8080 and tell me what LLM service is running on it."
"Check all these endpoints for LLM services: http://10.0.0.5:11434 http://10.0.0.6:8000 http://10.0.0.7:3000"
"Is there an Ollama instance running on my local network at 192.168.4.174?"
"Probe https://api.example.com and identify if it's using vLLM, TGI, or another inference server."
"List all the probe types that Julius supports for fingerprinting LLM services."
"Scan my staging AI endpoints with high concurrency and a 10-second timeout per probe."

Description:

It also supports isochrone generation to visualize travel-time boundaries and can generate static map images to visualize complex geographic data.

Global geocoding to convert addresses and place names to coordinates and vice versa
Points of interest (POI) search across millions of businesses, landmarks, and places worldwide
Multi-modal routing for driving, walking, and cycling with real-time traffic
Travel time matrices to analyze accessibility and optimize logistics
Route optimization to find the optimal visiting order for multiple stops (traveling salesman problem)
Map matching to snap GPS traces to the road network for clean route visualization
Isochrone generation to visualize areas reachable within specific time or distance constraints
Static map images to create visual representations of locations, routes, and geographic data
Offline geospatial calculations for distance, area, bearing, buffers, and spatial analysis without requiring API calls

get_vessel_position: Retrieves the real-time position of a vessel using its MMSI or IMO number.

get_vessel_details: Retrieves detailed information about a specific vessel using its MMSI or IMO number.

search_vessels: Searches for vessels by name, MMSI, IMO number, or vessel type.

get_vessels_in_area: Lists all vessels currently located within a specified geographic area (defined by latitude, longitude, and radius).

set_n2yo_api_key: Configures the N2YO API key for accessing satellite data.

query_satellites_natural: Answers natural language questions about satellites (e.g., "What satellites will be over France at 6:00 tonight?") with optional category filtering.

Description:
Analyzes HTTP response headers against OWASP security standards with recommendations

The OWASP Secure Headers Project (OSHP) provides information on HTTP response headers to increase the security of a web application.

The OSHP documentation project is an OWASP Lab Project and raises awareness of secure headers and their use.

What is OSHP?
The OSHP project provides explanations for the HTTP response headers that an application can use to increase the security of the application. Once set the HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

OSHP contains guidance and downloads on:

Response headers explanations and usage
Links to individual browser support
Guidance and best practices
Technical resources in the form of tools and documents
Code snippets to help working with HTTP security headers
Why use it?
The OSHP is a documentation project that explains the reasoning and usage of HTTP response headers. It is the go-to document for guidance and best practices; the information on HTTP response headers is the best advice, in one location, and is kept up to date.

A Model Context Protocol server for Scrapezy that enables AI models to extract structured data from websites.

Features
Tools
extract_structured_data - Extract structured data from a website
Takes URL and prompt as required parameters
Returns structured data extracted from the website based on the prompt
The prompt should clearly describe what data to extract from the website

A specialized code security server that brings Static Application Security Testing (SAST) directly into the AI development loop. It allows LLMs to scan code for vulnerabilities, secrets, and supply chain risks using standard or custom YAML rules. Key capabilities include generating Abstract Syntax Trees (AST) for code understanding and fetching findings from the Semgrep AppSec Platform. It is purpose-built to help AI agents "self-correct" their code until it passes security checks.

Search Keywords: Semgrep SAST, code security scanning, custom Semgrep rules, AST generation, secrets detection, supply chain security, AI-assisted remediation.

Use Case: "Scan the newly generated Python function for SQL injection vulnerabilities using Semgrep and apply the recommended fix if any are found."

Tools

Scan Code
security_check: Scan code for security vulnerabilities
semgrep_scan: Scan code files for security vulnerabilities with a given config string
semgrep_scan_with_custom_rule: Scan code files using a custom Semgrep rule
Understand Code
get_abstract_syntax_tree: Output the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) of code
Cloud Platform (login and Semgrep token required)
semgrep_findings: Fetch Semgrep findings from the Semgrep AppSec Platform API
Meta
supported_languages: Return the list of languages Semgrep supports
semgrep_rule_schema: Fetches the latest semgrep rule JSON Schema
Prompts
Reusable prompts to standardize common LLM interactions.

write_custom_semgrep_rule: Return a prompt to help write a Semgrep rule
Resources
Expose data and content to LLMs

semgrep://rule/schema: Specification of the Semgrep rule YAML syntax using JSON schema
semgrep://rule/{rule_id}/yaml: Full Semgrep rule in YAML format from the Semgrep registry

Sentry MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a specialized server that bridges Sentry's error monitoring with AI coding assistants, allowing tools like Cursor or Claude to intelligently query Sentry for debugging data (errors, traces, logs) and even trigger automated fixes via Sentry's AI agents, streamlining the root cause analysis for developers by providing immediate, relevant context from their applications. Essentially, it's an "API for AI" for Sentry, enabling natural language interaction with complex error data to speed up development.
The official "API for AI" for Sentry's error monitoring platform. It bridges production health data (errors, stack traces, breadcrumbs) to AI coding assistants. A standout feature is the integration with Seer, Sentry's native AI agent, which allows LLMs to trigger automated root cause analysis and retrieve fix recommendations. It supports 16+ tool calls for querying project health, release adoption, and performance degradation.

Search Keywords: Sentry error monitoring, Seer AI integration, automated root cause analysis, stack trace analysis, production debugging, performance monitoring, release health tracking.

Use Case: "Diagnose the most frequent crash in the 'Frontend-App' project from the last hour and propose a fix based on the stack trace."
Retrieving and analyzing application issues from Sentry.io.

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation that integrates with SerpApi for comprehensive search engine results and data extraction.
A search intelligence server that integrates with SerpApi to provide high-fidelity data extraction from major search engines (Google, Bing, Baidu, Yandex). Unlike standard scrapers, it handles proxy rotation, CAPTCHA solving, and returns clean, structured JSON. It includes specialized parsers for local results, shopping data, news, and knowledge graphs, ensuring the AI receives verified, up-to-date web information.

Search Keywords: SerpApi integration, Google Search results, search engine scraping, local SEO data, knowledge graph extraction, organic search results, search intelligence.

Use Case: "Retrieve the top 10 Google search results for 'best enterprise CRM 2026' and provide a structured summary of the featured snippet and organic rankings."

A Model Context Protocol server that provides Google Search via Serper. This server enables LLMs to get search result information from Google.
A lightweight, high-speed Model Context Protocol server that interfaces with the Serper.dev API to provide Google Search capabilities. It is optimized for speed and token efficiency, returning only the most relevant snippets, links, and "People Also Ask" data. This server is ideal for grounding LLMs in real-time facts without the overhead of full-page rendering.

Search Keywords: Serper.dev API, Google Search for LLMs, fast search retrieval, real-time fact checking, search snippet extraction, People Also Ask data.

Use Case: "Check Google for the current stock price of Tesla and the headline of their latest press release."

A specialized OSINT reconnaissance tool designed for username enumeration across 300+ social networks, forums, and websites. It uses asynchronous requests to quickly check for the existence of an account with a specific handle (e.g., checking if 'User123' exists on GitHub, Instagram, and Reddit simultaneously). It is a foundational tool for digital footprinting and identity correlation.

Search Keywords: username enumeration, Sherlock OSINT, social media footprinting, account discovery, digital identity mapping, username lookup.

Use Case: "Find every social media platform where the username 'dev_expert_99' is currently registered to build a professional profile map."

Description:
A dual-purpose cybersecurity server that combines Shodan’s internet-scale device intelligence with VirusTotal’s reputation analysis. It allows AI agents to perform deep infrastructure audits, identifying open ports, running services, and associated CVE vulnerabilities for any IP or domain. The integration with VirusTotal adds a layer of malware and URL reputation analysis, making it a comprehensive "security multi-tool" for SOC analysts.

Search Keywords: Shodan API, internet of things search, vulnerability assessment, VirusTotal malware analysis, open port discovery, CVE lookup, DNS intelligence, network alerting.

Use Case: "Analyze the IP address 8.8.8.8: check its open ports via Shodan and its reputation score via VirusTotal to determine if it is a safe DNS resolver."

Tools Provided:

host-info: Retrieves detailed information about a specific IP address (open ports, services, location).

dns-lookup: Resolves hostnames to IP addresses.

reverse-dns: Performs reverse DNS lookups for IP addresses.

domain-info: Retrieves DNS entries and subdomains for a specific domain.

search-host: Searches Shodan for hosts matching specific criteria/queries.

search-host-count: Returns the count of matching results for a query without full details.

list-ports: Lists all ports actively scanned by Shodan.

list-protocols: Lists all protocols available for scanning.

request-scan: Requests Shodan to scan specific IPs or networks.

get-scan-status: Checks the status of a submitted on-demand scan.

create-alert: Sets up network monitoring alerts for specific IPs or ports.

cve-lookup: Retrieves details about a specific CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures).

cpe-vuln-search: Searches for vulnerabilities associated with a specific CPE (Common Platform Enumeration).

virustotal-url-analysis: Analyzes URLs for security threats using VirusTotal.

virustotal-file-analysis: Checks file hashes (MD5/SHA) for malware presence.

virustotal-ip-analysis: Checks the reputation of an IP address via VirusTotal.

virustotal-domain-analysis: Analyzes domain reputation via VirusTotal.

A specialized communication server for Slack that operates in a "Stealth Mode," allowing AI agents to interact with channels and threads using user tokens (simulating human behavior) or standard bot OAuth. It features "Smart History," which can intelligently fetch message contexts based on dates or conversation density. It enables agents to search across private and public channels to synthesize internal knowledge or respond to queries within threads.

Search Keywords: Slack API for AI, conversation history extraction, Slack stealth mode, threaded message retrieval, internal knowledge search, Slack message automation.

Use Case: "Search the #engineering-leads channel for any discussion regarding 'database migration' from the last two weeks and summarize the decision."

Tools Provided:

conversations_history: Fetches messages from a channel or DM (supports "smart history" by date or message count).

conversations_replies: Retrieves a thread of messages from a specific conversation.

conversations_add_message: Posts a message to a channel, DM, or thread (disabled by default for safety; requires env var enablement).

conversations_search_messages: Searches for messages across channels, threads, and DMs using filters (date, user, content).

channels_list: Lists available channels (public, private, MPIM, IM) with optional sorting by popularity.
MCP server for interacting with Slack

Smuggler MCP allows you to use the Smuggler HTTP Request Smuggling testing tool through the MCP protocol. This integration offers Smuggler's capabilities to automated security testing processes or AI assistants.

A high-performance bridge between AI agents and the Splunk Cloud/Enterprise platform. It enables natural language interaction with machine data, allowing the AI to generate, explain, and optimize SPL (Splunk Processing Language) queries. Beyond searching logs, it can manage KV stores, inspect indexes, and retrieve metadata about hosts and sourcetypes, turning the AI into a virtual SOC or IT operations assistant.

Search Keywords: Splunk SPL generation, log analysis AI, KV store management, Splunk Cloud API, machine data intelligence, ITSI monitoring, security investigation.

Use Case: "Ask Splunk: 'Show me all failed login attempts in the last hour grouped by source IP,' and then explain the resulting SPL query to me."
This tool provides a set of capabilities for searching Splunk data, managing KV stores, and accessing Splunk resources through an intuitive interface.

Features
Splunk Search: Execute Splunk searches with natural language queries

Index Management: List and inspect Splunk indexes

User Management: View and manage Splunk users

KV Store Operations: Create, list, and manage KV store collections

Async Support: Built with async/await patterns for better performance

Detailed Logging: Comprehensive logging with emoji indicators for better visibility

SSL Configuration: Flexible SSL verification options for different security requirements

Enhanced Debugging: Detailed connection and error logging for troubleshooting

Comprehensive Testing: Unit tests covering all major functionality

Error Handling: Robust error handling with appropriate status codes


The following tools are available via the MCP interface:

Tools Management
list_tools
Lists all available MCP tools with their descriptions and 

Health Check

Returns a list of available Splunk apps to verify connectivity
ping

A "data-as-SQL" server that allows AI agents to query 100+ cloud services and APIs using standard SQL syntax. By connecting to a local Steampipe PostgreSQL database, the AI can treat cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP), SaaS tools (GitHub, Slack), and security logs as relational tables. This allows for complex joins across disparate platforms (e.g., joining AWS EC2 data with GitHub commit history) to identify security gaps or resource waste.

Search Keywords: Steampipe SQL, infrastructure as code query, multi-cloud SQL, AWS inventory SQL, SaaS data integration, PostgreSQL cloud wrapper, resource auditing.

Use Case: "Run a SQL query via Steampipe to find all AWS S3 buckets that are publicly accessible and cross-reference them with the 'owner' tag from our GitHub organization data."
Steampipe MCP server allows an AI to query cloud services and APIs using SQL. It connects to a local Steampipe PostgreSQL database, so the model can run SQL queries to inspect infrastructure, cloud resources, and SaaS data (like AWS, Azure, GitHub, etc.) through Steampipe’s unified interface.

Stripe MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open-source server that enables AI agents and LLMs (like Claude) to securely connect to and interact with the Stripe API. It allows AI to manage, create, and retrieve data for customers, products, subscriptions, and invoices, as well as search Stripe documentation directly from development environments.

RAG Description: A secure financial gateway that connects AI agents to the Stripe API. It allows models to perform core business operations such as managing customer records, creating subscriptions, and generating payment links. Beyond transactional tasks, it features a specialized "Search Stripe Resources" tool that lets AI agents query the official Stripe documentation to ensure any code or logic they generate aligns with the latest API versions and security best practices.

Search Keywords: Stripe API integration, automated billing AI, subscription management, payment link generator, customer data RAG, invoice automation, Stripe docs search.

Enumerate subdomains using Subfinder (fast passive discovery). Subfinder is extremely fast for passive subdomain discovery using multiple passive sources. Use this for quick subdomain enumeration when speed is important. Supports recursive enumeration, source filtering, and rate limiting.
A high-speed subdomain discovery tool focused on passive enumeration. By querying over 30 external sources (such as crt.sh, Censys, and VirusTotal) without ever interacting with the target's infrastructure directly, it provides a fast and "stealthy" map of a company's digital attack surface. It is highly optimized for performance, supporting recursive discovery and automatic wildcard elimination to ensure high-fidelity results.

Search Keywords: passive subdomain discovery, attack surface mapping, ProjectDiscovery, stealth reconnaissance, DNS enumeration, bug bounty automation, infrastructure mapping.

Use Case: "Perform a rapid passive scan of 'example.com' to identify any exposed development or staging subdomains without triggering active security alerts."
Author: ProjectDiscovery

Description:
The Tavily MCP Server is a lightweight, production ready server that brings live web capabilities search, crawling, and structured data extraction to any AI assistant through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Once deployed, MCP compatible clients like Claude, Cursor, or custom LLM agents can issue natural language requests that trigger real time search, extraction, and crawling workflows. Results are returned as clean, structured context: summaries, extracted data, metadata, and more, ready to be injected directly as context for AI agents. Once deployed, MCP compatible clients, such as Claude, Cursor, or custom LLM agents, can issue natural language requests that trigger real time search, extraction, and crawling workflows. Results are returned as clean, structured context: summaries, extracted data, metadata, and more. Ready to be injected directly into the models reasoning loop. Built for easy integration, Tavily MCP empowers AI developers to securely, scalably, and with minimal overhead infuse external web knowledge into their agents.
A production-ready web search and data extraction server specifically designed for LLMs. Unlike standard search engines, Tavily returns "AI-ready" context: clean, structured text, direct answers with supporting evidence, and specialized news results. It includes advanced features like search_depth (Basic vs. Advanced) and domain filtering, allowing agents to perform deep research while minimizing token noise from irrelevant web clutter.

Search Keywords: AI search engine, structured web extraction, Tavily API, real-time news RAG, content cleaning for LLMs, search depth optimization.

Use Case: "Research the latest consensus on 2026 solid-state battery breakthroughs and provide a structured summary with citations from top-tier technical publications."

The Terraform MCP Server (Model Context Protocol Server) is a service developed by HashiCorp that acts as a bridge between AI models (like AI-powered code editors or chat assistants) and the live data of the Terraform ecosystem. This allows AI to generate accurate, up-to-date, and context-aware Terraform code for infrastructure as code (IaC) development, instead of relying on potentially outdated general training data.
A specialized server that links generic LLMs to the live Terraform Registry and local Ops environments. Developed by HashiCorp, it provides AI assistants with real-time access to provider documentation, module metadata, and Sentinel policy sets. This "live context" prevents the AI from generating deprecated HCL code and allows it to suggest infrastructure changes that comply with specific organizational security benchmarks.

Search Keywords: Terraform Registry API, Infrastructure as Code AI, HCL code generation, provider documentation search, Sentinel policy audit, HashiCorp cloud automation.

Use Case: "Help me refactor this AWS VPC module using the latest verified modules from the Terraform Registry, ensuring it follows the CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark."

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI agents with seamless access to TomTom's location services. It enables LLMs to integrate precise geolocation data into workflows, offering capabilities for search, routing, real-time traffic updates, and static or dynamic map generation.
It is a professional-grade location intelligence server that exposes TomTom’s global maps, routing, and traffic APIs. It allows AI agents to perform complex geospatial tasks like "fuzzy search" for addresses (tolerant of typos), calculating "reachable ranges" (isochrones) based on a time budget, and generating dynamic map visualizations. It is highly valued for logistics and smart mobility applications where real-time traffic data is critical.

Search Keywords: TomTom location services, isochrone generation, real-time traffic API, multi-stop routing, fuzzy address search, geospatial visualization, POIs search.

Use Case: "Calculate the most efficient route for three delivery stops in downtown Chicago, accounting for current traffic incidents, and visualize the reachable area for a 15-minute return window."

Tools Provided:

tomtom-geocode: Converts addresses into geographic coordinates (forward geocoding) with global coverage.

tomtom-reverse-geocode: Converts GPS coordinates into human-readable addresses.

tomtom-fuzzy-search: Performs intelligent search for addresses and places with typo tolerance.

tomtom-poi-search: Finds specific business categories and points of interest.

tomtom-nearby: Discovers services and POIs within a specific radius of a location.

tomtom-routing: Calculates optimal routes between two locations.

tomtom-waypoint-routing: Plans multi-stop routes with optimized waypoints.

tomtom-reachable-range: Calculates the reachable area (isochrone) from a point based on time or distance budgets.

tomtom-traffic: Retrieves real-time traffic incident data and details.

tomtom-dynamic-map: Generates advanced map visualizations with custom markers, routes, and traffic overlays (requires specific configuration).

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that integrates the Trivy security scanner. It enables AI assistants to perform automated security assessments by scanning container images and remote Git repositories to identify Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) and exposed secrets.

Tools Provided:

scan_container_image: Scans a specified container image (e.g., nginx:latest) for vulnerabilities. It allows filtering by severity level (UNKNOWN, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) and includes a 3-minute timeout to handle large image pulls.

scan_git_repository: Scans a remote Git repository URL to identify both software vulnerabilities and hardcoded secrets, with customizable severity thresholds.

A comprehensive Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that integrates data from IRIS, USGS, and GNSS/GPS networks to provide global earthquake monitoring and analysis. It enables real-time tracking of seismic activity, crustal deformation monitoring, and advanced risk assessment through multi-source data validation.

It is a comprehensive seismic monitoring server that integrates data from the USGS, IRIS, and GNSS/GPS networks. It enables AI agents to track global earthquake feeds and perform advanced risk assessments, such as "b-value" calculations and hazard probability modeling. It can also retrieve "ShakeMaps" to visualize ground motion intensity and monitor GNSS stations for crustal deformation that might precede significant seismic events.

Search Keywords: USGS earthquake API, seismic risk analysis, GNSS crustal deformation, IRIS waveform data, ShakeMap intensity, seismic hazard assessment.

Use Case: "Analyze recent seismic activity in the Cascadia Subduction Zone and retrieve the waveform data for any event over magnitude 4.5 in the last week."

Tools Provided:

analyze-seismic-activity: Performs comprehensive seismic risk analysis for a specific region, including statistical analysis, b-value calculation, and hazard probability assessment.

fetch-waveform: Retrieves seismic waveform data for specific earthquake events from the IRIS database.

monitor-gnss-displacement: Monitors GPS/GNSS stations for unusual crustal movements that may indicate seismic activity.

get-usgs-earthquakes: Retrieves recent earthquake data from USGS real-time feeds.

search-usgs-earthquakes: Searches the USGS earthquake database using custom parameters.

get-usgs-shakemap: Retrieves ShakeMap intensity and ground motion data for specific earthquake events.

get-seismic-hazard: Provides probabilistic seismic hazard assessments for specific locations (requires valid site class or VS30 value).

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects AI assistants to the VariFlight API for real-time aviation data. It allows LLMs to retrieve comprehensive flight information, including schedules, status, airport weather forecasts, and aircraft tracking, enabling intelligent travel planning and monitoring.
it is a real-time aviation intelligence server that connects AI assistants to the VariFlight API. It provides high-precision data on flight status, airport weather, and aircraft positioning. Unique features include the "Flight Happiness Index," which scores flight comfort (seat pitch, entertainment, meals), and "Transfer Info" for optimizing complex itineraries. It allows agents to track specific aircraft using tail numbers for precise arrival planning.

Search Keywords: real-time flight tracking, VariFlight API, airport weather forecast, flight happiness index, tail number tracking, aviation data RAG, itinerary search.

Use Case: "Find the comfort rating for UA123 today and check the 3-day weather forecast for its destination airport to help plan my ground transportation."

Tools Provided:

searchFlightsByDepArr: Searches for flights between two specific airports using IATA codes.

searchFlightsByNumber: Retrieves detailed information for a specific flight using its flight number.

getFlightTransferInfo: Finds available flight transfer options between two cities.

flightHappinessIndex: Provides comfort metrics for a flight, such as meal options, entertainment, and seat pitch.

getRealtimeLocationByAnum: Tracks the real-time location and status of an aircraft using its tail (registration) number.

getFutureWeatherByAirport: Retrieves the 3-day weather forecast for a specific airport.

searchFlightItineraries: Searches for purchasable flight itineraries, including pricing and schedule details.

getTodayDate: A utility to retrieve the current date to ensure accurate time-based queries.

Get the most frequent values per each log field (facets) from VictoriaLogs.
A discovery tool that identifies the most frequent values for every available log field. It helps users understand the distribution of their data by calculating "facets"—a breakdown of field values and their occurrence counts. This is essential for identifying top error sources, common user agents, or the most active IP addresses within a dataset.

Search Keywords: log facets, field value distribution, top values analysis, log data discovery, observability facets, high-cardinality analysis.

Use Case: "Show me the top 10 values for the status_code field to see which HTTP errors are occurring most frequently."

Get available field names from logs matching the query in VictoriaLogs.
Discovery tools for navigating the schemaless nature of VictoriaLogs. Field Names retrieves all keys currently indexed in the logs (e.g., user_id, ip), while Field Values provides the unique values associated with a specific key. These tools are critical for building dynamic search filters and exploring unknown datasets.

Search Keywords: log schema discovery, unique field values, list log fields, dynamic filter building, metadata exploration, schemaless indexing.

Use Case: "List all unique container_name values present in the logs from the last 10 minutes to verify which pods are currently reporting."

Discovery tools for navigating the schemaless nature of VictoriaLogs. These tools are critical for building dynamic search filters and exploring unknown datasets.

Search Keywords: log schema discovery, unique field values, list log fields, dynamic filter building, metadata exploration, schemaless indexing.

Use Case: "List all unique container_name values present in the logs from the last 10 minutes to verify which pods are currently reporting."

Analyze log hits over time in VictoriaLogs for time-series analysis.
A time-series analysis tool that calculates the number of log entries (hits) matching a query over a specified time range. It returns a distribution of log counts broken down by a time step, making it ideal for visualizing spikes in log volume, identifying service outages, or correlating log frequency with other metrics.

Search Keywords: log hits analysis, time-series log count, log volume tracking, error spike detection, query hits distribution, observability trends.

Use Case: "Plot the total number of logs with level:error over the last 24 hours with 5-minute granularity to identify when the system was most unstable."

The primary interface for interacting with VictoriaLogs using LogsQL, a high-performance query language designed for full-text search and analytical pipes. This tool can query billions of logs across structured and unstructured data, supporting complex filtering, regex matching, and data transformations. It is the foundation for log exploration and ad-hoc debugging.

Search Keywords: LogsQL query, log exploration, full-text log search, regex log filtering, structured logs analysis, ad-hoc debugging, data transformation.

Use Case: "Find all logs containing the word 'timeout' in the 'prod-gateway' service and return the last 100 entries."

Run a stats query over a time range in VictoriaLogs to get time-series statistics.
These tools run powerful aggregations and metrics calculations on log data. The Stats Tool provides instant aggregations (like total count, average, or max), while the Stats Range Tool returns time-series statistics (like a moving average of response times). They transform raw logs into actionable metrics without needing a separate monitoring system.

Search Keywords: log statistics, LogsQL stats pipe, log-to-metrics conversion, time-series aggregations, average calculation logs, metric extraction.

Use Case: "Calculate the 95th percentile of the request_duration field for all successful API calls over the past hour."

These tools run powerful aggregations and metrics calculations on log data. Use Case: "Calculate the 95th percentile of the request_duration field for all successful API calls over the past hour."

Get stream field names from log streams matching the query in VictoriaLogs.
These tools manage and explore Log Streams, which are logical buckets of logs grouped by constant identifiers (like app, host, or instance). VictoriaLogs uses streams to optimize disk compression and query speed. These tools allow you to list active streams, identify which fields are designated as stream identifiers, and explore their unique values to optimize your storage architecture.

Search Keywords: log stream management, stream field discovery, data compression optimization, _stream_id lookup, log grouping, high-cardinality stream analysis.

Use Case: "List all stream fields to ensure that high-cardinality fields like trace_id are not accidentally being used as stream identifiers."

Get list of available tenants in VictoriaLogs.
A multi-tenancy management tool that retrieves a list of all available tenants within the VictoriaLogs instance. Each tenant is identified by an AccountID and a ProjectID, allowing for strict data isolation between different teams, environments, or customers. This tool ensures that AI agents can correctly scope their queries to the intended tenant.

Search Keywords: multi-tenancy VictoriaLogs, tenant isolation, AccountID ProjectID list, RBAC for logs, data scoping, organizational log separation.

Use Case: "List all active tenants to find the correct AccountID for the 'Staging' environment before running a compliance audit."

This tool queries VirusTotal's database for domain analysis results, providing threat intelligence about domains associated with malicious activity, phishing, malware distribution, or other security threats. A deep-dive reconnaissance tool for domain names. It aggregates WHOIS data, SSL certificate history, DNS records (A, AAAA, MX, NS), and a full list of known subdomains. This tool is particularly effective for mapping out an adversary's infrastructure and identifying "parked" domains that may be part of a larger phishing campaign.

Search Keywords: domain threat intelligence, subdomain enumeration, SSL certificate history, WHOIS data retrieval, passive DNS domain mapping.

Use Case: "Perform a domain report on suspicious-updates.com to find all its subdomains and see who registered it."

Get VirusTotal analysis report for a file by hash (MD5, SHA1, or SHA256).
A diagnostic tool that retrieves existing analysis data for a file based on its unique hash (MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256). It provides a consensus report from 70+ antivirus engines, static metadata (digital signatures, headers), and behavior summaries from various sandbox environments. This tool is critical for verifying known malware without requiring the actual file to be uploaded, thus preserving data privacy.

Search Keywords: file hash detection, SHA256 malware check, antivirus consensus, static file metadata, malware behavior report.

Use Case: "Identify if the hash d41d8cd98f... has been flagged as malicious by any top-tier security vendors in the last 6 months."

Get the results of a VirusTotal analysis by its analysis ID. A retrieval tool used to poll the results of a specific scan request using its unique Analysis ID. After a "Scan URL" task is submitted, this tool allows the agent to wait and then fetch the completed multi-engine report, providing the final detection tally and technical details once the scanning engines have finished their work.

Search Keywords: fetch scan results, Analysis ID lookup, scan polling tool, VirusTotal task status, completed analysis retrieval.

Use Case: "Retrieve the final detection results for the scan I just initiated using the analysis ID u-1a2b3c4d...."

This tool queries VirusTotal's database for IP address analysis results, providing threat intelligence about IPs associated with malicious activity, command & control servers, botnets, DDoS attacks, or other security threats. A network intelligence tool that provides a reputation profile for a specific IP address. The report includes geolocation data (Country, ASN), a history of malicious URLs hosted at that address, and any malware samples that have communicated with it. It also details the "Passive DNS" history, showing which domains have historically pointed to that IP.

Search Keywords: IP reputation audit, passive DNS history, malicious host profiling, ASN intelligence, IP-malware correlation.

Use Case: "Analyze the IP 103.25.x.x to see if it has been associated with any Command & Control (C2) activity in the past."

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects AI assistants to the VirusTotal API for comprehensive security analysis. It allows agents to retrieve detailed reports on URLs, files, IPs, and domains, automatically fetching relevant relationship data (such as communicating files, threat actors, and historical DNS records) to provide a complete security overview.


Tools Provided:

get_url_report: Retrieves a comprehensive security analysis for a specific URL, including scan results, redirects, and communicating files.

get_file_report: Retrieves a detailed analysis of a file using its hash (MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256), including behavior, detection results, and network connections.

Purpose You can directly ask the AI to check if a domain is available, who owns it, when it was registered, and other important details. No need to go to browser and search.

What is a WHOIS Lookup? A WHOIS lookup is the process of querying a WHOIS database to retrieve registration details about a domain name, IP address, or autonomous system. It helps users find out who owns a domain, when it was registered, when it expires, and other important details.

What Information Can a WHOIS Lookup Provide?

When you perform a WHOIS lookup, you can retrieve details such as:

Domain Name – The specific domain queried
Registrar Name – The company managing the domain registration (e.g., GoDaddy, Namecheap)
Registrant Details – The name, organization, and contact details of the domain owner (unless protected by WHOIS privacy)
Registration & Expiry Date – When the domain was registered and when it will expire
Name Servers – The DNS servers the domain is using
Domain Status – Active, expired, locked, or pending deletion
Contact Information – Administrative, technical, and billing contacts (if not hidden)
Available Tools
Tool	Description
whois_domain	Looksup whois information about the domain
whois_tld	Looksup whois information about the Top Level Domain (TLD)
whois_ip	Looksup whois information about the IP
whois_as	Looksup whois information about the Autonomous System Number (ASN)

Key Features
All the features you need from WhoisXMLAPI in an MCP server
Domain WHOIS lookups and historical data
DNS analysis and monitoring
IP geolocation and network intelligence
Email verification and domain categorization
SSL certificate analysis
Threat intelligence and security insights
Reverse DNS, IP, and WHOIS searches

A financial intelligence tool that uses the yfinance library to provide real-time and historical market data. It features advanced capabilities like generating professional financial charts (Candlestick, VWAP, Volume Profile) as base64-encoded images. It also retrieves company financials, analyst recommendations, and sector-wide "top performer" lists.

Search Keywords: Yahoo Finance API, real-time stock data, candlestick chart generator, VWAP analysis, financial statement retrieval.

Use Case: "Show me a 1-month candlestick chart for AAPL with a VWAP overlay and list the latest analyst upgrades."

Tools
yfinance_get_ticker_info

Retrieve stock data including company info, financials, trading metrics and governance data.
Inputs:
symbol (string): The stock symbol.
yfinance_get_ticker_news

Fetches recent news articles related to a specific stock symbol with title, content, and source details.
Inputs:
symbol (string): The stock symbol.
yfinance_search

Fetches and organizes search results from Yahoo Finance, including stock quotes and news articles.
Inputs:
query (string): The search query (ticker symbol or company name).
search_type (string): Type of search results to retrieve (options: "all", "quotes", "news").
yfinance_get_top

Get top entities (ETFs, mutual funds, companies, growth companies, or performing companies) in a sector.
Inputs:
sector (string): The sector to get.
top_type (string): Type of top companies to retrieve (options: "top_etfs", "top_mutual_funds", "top_companies", "top_growth_companies", "top_performing_companies").
top_n (number, optional): Number of top entities to retrieve (default 10).
yfinance_get_price_history

Fetch historical price data for a given stock symbol over a specified period and interval. Can return data as a markdown table or generate professional financial charts using mplfinance, including candlestick charts with volume bars, VWAP overlays, and volume profile analysis.
Inputs:
symbol (string): The stock symbol.
period (string, optional): Time period to retrieve data for (e.g. '1d', '1mo', '1y'). Default is '1mo'.
interval (string, optional): Data interval frequency (e.g. '1d', '1h', '1m'). Default is '1d'.
chart_type (string, optional): Type of chart to generate. If not specified, returns price data as markdown table. Options:
"price_volume": Candlestick chart with volume bars
"vwap": Volume Weighted Average Price chart with VWAP overlay
"volume_profile": Candlestick chart with volume profile showing volume distribution by price level (displayed as a histogram on the right side)
Output:
If chart_type is not specified: Returns historical price data as a markdown table
If chart_type is specified: Returns a base64-encoded WebP image for efficient token usage

A YARA-based Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that integrates with AI assistants to perform automated malware scanning and threat analysis.
A security-focused server that integrates YARA, the industry-standard tool for pattern matching and malware classification. It allows AI agents to scan files or raw code strings against custom or community-sourced rule sets (e.g., ThreatFlux). The server supports dynamic rule management, hex views for binary analysis, and string extraction, turning an LLM into an automated malware triage assistant.

Search Keywords: YARA rule scanning, malware pattern matching, YaraFlux API, automated threat analysis, YARA rule compiler, IoC detection.

Use Case: "Scan this uploaded executable against the 'Ransomware' YARA ruleset and provide a detailed report of any pattern matches found."
Features & Tools Provided:
• scan_file: Scans files at a specified path against the loaded YARA ruleset.
• scan_string: Analyzes raw text strings or code snippets for malicious patterns.
• compile_rule: Validates and compiles new YARA rules to ensure syntax correctness.
• list_rules: Retrieves a list of all currently active YARA rules in the system.
• add_rule / remove_rule: Dynamically manages the rule database without restarting the server.

Search Keywords: YouTube Data API v3, video transcript retrieval, channel analytics tool, playlist management API, AI video research.

A Model Context Protocol server that enables retrieval of transcripts from YouTube videos. This server provides direct access to video captions and subtitles through a simple interface. A specialized utility for unlocking the knowledge contained within YouTube videos. These servers (like jkawamoto/mcp-youtube-transcript) fetch subtitles and captions, often supporting pagination to handle very long videos that exceed LLM token limits. Some advanced versions include a Whisper fallback to generate transcripts for videos that lack official closed captioning, enabling the AI to "read" almost any video on the platform.

Search Keywords: YouTube transcript extractor, video caption API, YouTube metadata retrieval, Whisper transcript fallback, video summarization AI.

Use Case: "Retrieve the full transcript of this 2-hour technical lecture and provide a summary of the code examples mentioned between the 30-minute and 45-minute marks."

Single-packet scanning using ZMap. ZMap is a fast single-packet network scanner designed for Internet-wide network surveys. It can perform a complete scan of the IPv4 address space in under 45 minutes. Use for Internet-scale network surveys and large-scale port scanning. A high-speed, single-packet network scanner designed specifically for Internet-wide surveys. Unlike Nmap, which maintains state for every connection, ZMap uses cyclic multiplicative groups to scan the entire public IPv4 address space on a single port in under 45 minutes from a gigabit connection. This makes it the go-to tool for large-scale security research, vulnerability impact assessment, and identifying the global footprint of IoT devices.

Search Keywords: Internet-wide network survey, IPv4 address space scanner, stateless network scanning, large-scale port discovery, security research tools.

Use Case: "Perform a scan of the entire Internet to identify all publicly accessible servers running a specific version of a vulnerable service on port 443."
Author: zmap

httpx MCP enables seamless integration of httpx's capabilities to scan and analyze HTTP/HTTPS services into MCP-compatible applications and AI-powered workflow systems. This bridge allows you to detect active web services, extract valuable information about web servers, and identify technologies in use through a standardized protocol, making it easier to incorporate into automated security testing pipelines or AI assistant capabilities.
httpx MCP supports the following parameters:

target: A list of domain names to scan for HTTP and HTTPS services (required)
ports: Custom ports to scan (optional, defaults to standard HTTP/HTTPS ports)
probes: List of probe options to extract specific information (optional)
Available Probe Options
status-code: Display response status-code
content-length: Display response content-length
content-type: Display response content-type
location: Display response redirect location
favicon: Display mmh3 hash for '/favicon.ico' file
hash: Display response body hash (supported: md5, mmh3, simhash, sha1, sha256, sha512)
jarm: Display jarm fingerprint hash
response-time: Display response time
line-count: Display response body line count
word-count: Display response body word count
title: Display page title
body-preview: Display first N characters of response body (default 100)
web-server: Display server name
tech-detect: Display technology in use based on wappalyzer dataset
method: Display HTTP request method
websocket: Display server using websocket
ip: Display host IP
cname: Display host CNAME
extract-fqdn: Get domain and subdomains from response body and header
asn: Display host ASN information
cdn: Display CDN/WAF in use (default true)
probe: Display probe status


Example conversation with an AI assistant:

User: Can you check what web technologies are used by example.com?
AI: I'll scan example.com to identify the web technologies in use.
